title: Beykent University

details: Supported workshops, events and conferences; produced visuals and multimedia for web and print; managed mailings, website and internal database; documentation, photography and video editing; press kits, media relations, and hosting visiting speakers.

details: Founding core-team member of a bilingual arts & design publication that grew into a design platform. Led content, communications, graphic design, social media and the website, and supported events from Design Week Turkey to the Venice Biennale, Milan Design Week and Maison & Objet. Art director of Atlas, empowering local and refugee women in south-eastern Turkey through design.

Created social content and supported major events (Istanbul Development Dialogues, Istanbul Innovation Days, Social Good Summit), with monitoring and analytics. Helped shift the bureau toward an Instagram-first, warmer tone.

Produced daily social content and tracked global branding and design trends; built a persona and communications strategy and drafted the Content Policy Guidelines, shifting the brand's online voice toward friendlier, more engaging content.

details:

Also led ILO's public campaign against xenophobia toward migrant workers across four Southern African countries (Seychelles, South Africa, Zimbabwe, Lesotho). The #candid project won Gold at PR Awards Asia and at the Davos Communications Awards.

title: Marmara Urban Forum '25
role: Workshop Host — Memetivism for Urban Justice
details: Memes, often characterized by their humorous and viral nature, have become a ubiquitous form of online expression, shaping the way we engage with and understand contemporary issues. Their potential as a tool for political expression and advocacy has been increasingly recognized, turning humor into a medium for raising awareness, critiquing power, and imagining alternatives. Meme-tivism is a methodology that builds on this potential, developed by Sinem Görücü and feministdesign.co, which leverages the participatory, visual, and creative qualities of meme culture to explore social and political issues. At MARUF, Meme-tivism turns its focus to urban justice, inviting participants to a hands-on session where they co-generate playful yet critical memes that spark dialogue, reflection, and collective imagining, while weaving in participants’ experiences and insights from MARUF program discussions.

title: Walk21 Lisbon
role: Walkshop — Creating Alternative Ways of Assessing Walkability
details:
This workshop aims to bring a new perspective to how we understand and measure walkability in urban spaces. Walking is a very personal and emotional experience. Yet the traditional metrics have long focused on physical and safety aspects of pedestrian environments mostly focusing on sidewalk availability. This approach overlooks the complex, multifaceted experiences of those who navigate these spaces daily. The purpose of this workshop is to introduce and explore a broad spectrum of possible indicators that encapsulate the emotional, sensory, and personalized experiences of walkability. 

Participants will come up with new, creative indicators other than physical conditions for walkability assessments from their own experiences.  For example: “Percentage of Advertisements Exposed per Eyesight”, “Culinary Smell Diversity”, “Sensory and Cognitive Input Density”. Later, these indicators will be published as an open source document and people will be able to add their own indicators or assess their daily routes with these indicators.

By integrating KPIs related to smell, visual and auditory experiences, interactive elements, and emotional impacts such as a sense of belonging, joy, and aesthetic appreciation we aim to deepen the conversation around urban experience. These alternative KPIs offer a more nuanced understanding of walkability, emphasizing the creation of spaces that not only facilitate movement but also enhance quality of life and foster social connections.

title: Izmir Metropolitan Municipality
role: Panels & Talks Coordinator — Good Design İzmir_9
details: Curated and coordinated the panel programme for Good Design İzmir 2024: Dialogue. Developed the conceptual framework, shaped session titles and formats, and coordinated speakers across architecture, urbanism, public space, design, art, sound, photography, food, memory, care and VR — a bilingual programme with local and international practitioners, academics, artists and institutions.

title: Transparency Summer School
details:
Transparency Summer School is a two week intensive governance programme hosted by Transparency International Turkey. Bringing together 100 undergraduate students from diverse academic disciplines across Türkiye, the programme explored the principles of transparency, accountability, participation and integrity through the lens of anti-corruption and good governance.

The curriculum combined lectures, workshops, film screenings and panel discussions led by academics, journalists, legal experts and civil society practitioners. Topics included ethics, anti-corruption policies, political finance, investigative journalism, conflict of interest, compliance in the private sector, data literacy, local governance, tax justice and income inequality. The interdisciplinary format encouraged critical thinking and practical dialogue on strengthening democratic institutions and public integrity.

The programme provided an opportunity to engage with peers from across the country while developing a deeper understanding of governance challenges and the role of transparency, accountability and civic participation in building more resilient public institutions.

title: OSCE - Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe
details: Worked as an Interpreter for Short Term Observers during the first and second rounds of the 2023 Turkish Parliamentary and Presidential Elections in Aydın and Muğla provinces.

title: Business for Goals
details: Developed the growth strategy for Business for Goals, a platform bridging the business community and the Sustainable Development Goals in Türkiye.

details: Worked with SUN Tekstil (INDITEX's largest supplier in Europe) on sustainability communications, proposing a Care for People standard for INDITEX alongside the existing Care for Planet. Also wrote an article on SUN Textile's sustainability efforts for Just Style magazine.

title: Feminist Design

title: Izmir Metropolitan Municipality

Ran 2016–2019 with 200+ students.

title: Teyit FACT-ORY
details: Hackathon against online disinformation. Built a Twitter bot to make reverse-image fact-checking easier and curb the spread of recycled or miscaptioned images.

title: IFY- Ideathon for Youth
details: 24-hour ideathon for high-school students on SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation & Infrastructure) at Impact Hub Istanbul. Helped plan and run the event and mentored participants from across Turkey.

title: All Tech Is Human
role: Design Lead — AI & Human Rights report
details: Major report by the New York NGO All Tech Is Human on AI's potential and threats in a democratic society. Led the design team and contributed to the Autonomous Weapons chapter.

title: Humboldt University
details: By Bulent Somay

title: Good Design Izmir_8
details:
İçinde yaşadığımız tasarlanmış çevreler, çoğunlukla bir ‘ortalama kullanıcı’ ideali üzerinden kurgulanarak küçük bir azınlığa hizmet ederken birçoğumuzun ihtiyaç ve isteklerini göz ardı ediyor. Tasarladıkları süreçleri şekillendiren kabuller, önyargılar ve güç dinamiklerine bağlı olarak, gündelik hayatımızın farklı noktalarında bu çevreler ve ürünlere uyum sağlayabilmek için sürekli inovatif yaklaşımlar ve hack’leme gibi pratikler geliştirmek durumunda kalıyoruz. Bu atölyede katılımcılar, tasarım ve veri adaleti literatürlerinin de kılavuzluğunda; tasarım, güç ve sosyal adalet arasındaki çok katmanlı ilişkiyi kesişkenlik perspektifinden inceleyerek gündelik nesnelerin tasarımındaki tahakküm pratiklerini çözümleme imkanı bulacaklar ve bir tasarım yöntemi olarak ‘hackleme’yi kullanarak kuramsal ve deneyimsel bilgileri spekülatif fikirlere dönüştürüp çeşitli modelleme tekniklerini kullanarak tasarımlarını prototipleyecekler. 
Atölye Hedefleri: Tasarımdaki kesişkenliğin ele alınması, tasarım tahakkümünün tanınabilmesi, tasarım ve veri adaleti literatürünün anlaşılması, ‘hackleme’ yoluyla spekülatif fikirlerin üretilmesi, ‘ortalama kullanıcı’ normuna karşı prototipler geliştirilmesi.
